description: REST API design and implementation standards for Sovereign-generated apps.

versioning.rule: Never remove or rename a field in a v1 response — add new fields, deprecate old ones
versioning.sovereign_current: Sovereign API routes are unversioned internally — versioning applies to public-facing APIs in generated apps

error_format.description: All error responses must follow this consistent structure

schema.message: string — human-readable description with the fix where possible

example.message: The 'email' field must be a valid email address

status_codes.200: OK — successful GET, PATCH, or action
status_codes.201: Created — successful POST that created a resource
status_codes.204: No Content — successful DELETE with no response body
status_codes.400: Bad Request — client sent invalid or missing fields
status_codes.401: Unauthorized — missing or invalid auth token
status_codes.403: Forbidden — authenticated but not authorized for this resource
status_codes.404: Not Found — resource does not exist or is not visible to this user
status_codes.422: Unprocessable Entity — valid format but invalid business logic (e.g. past date)
status_codes.429: Too Many Requests — rate limit exceeded (must include Retry-After header)
status_codes.500: Internal Server Error — unexpected server-side failure

rate_limiting.rule: Every public endpoint must be rate limited
response_headers: X-RateLimit-Limit: <max> — optional but helpful for API consumers

sovereign_limits.auth: 5 requests / 15 minutes per IP (stricter for auth endpoints)

pagination.strategy: Cursor-based pagination preferred for real-time data; offset for admin dashboards

response.next_cursor: string or null (null = no more pages)

request_rules: Validate method first — return 405 immediately for wrong method before any processing
Rate limit check second — return 429 before parsing body
Auth check third — return 401/403 before touching database
Input validation fourth — return 400 with specific field errors before any mutation
Business logic last — only execute when all checks pass
Always use idempotency keys for mutation endpoints that create payments or critical records
response_rules: Never return internal error messages in production — log internally, return generic message externally
Never include secrets, tokens, or passwords in API responses
Set Cache-Control: no-store on all auth and user-data endpoints
Set Content-Type: application/json on all JSON responses
All timestamps must be ISO 8601 format in UTC: 2026-03-21T14:30:00Z
sovereign_specific: All api/ routes in Sovereign use .js extensions on relative imports (Node ESM requirement)
export const config is placed after all import statements
Security audit comment block at top of every route file
Body parser size limit: export const config = { api: { bodyParser: { sizeLimit: '10mb' } } } for generation routes
